findings.title: Query contract still says cursor continuation is post-access only
evidence: docs/contracts/QUERY_CONTRACT.md performance model
docs/design/0.196-sqlite-comparison-audit/implementation-results.md
findings.recommended_fix: Rewrite the query contract performance model to separate semantic cursor guarantees from route-dependent execution and to mention pushed/limit-stopped ordered reads as an optimization.
acceptance_criteria: The query contract no longer states that cursor continuation is always post-access.
The contract states that public read admission cannot be bypassed by runtime fallback.
The contract preserves stable cursor semantics independent of route.
findings.suggested_patch_prompt: Update docs/contracts/QUERY_CONTRACT.md for 0.196: separate semantic cursor guarantees from route-dependent execution, document pushed/limit-stopped ordered reads as an optimization only, and state runtime fallback cannot bypass public read admission.

findings.title: Cursor historical note can be mistaken for current implementation
evidence: docs/contracts/CURSOR.md
findings.recommended_fix: Add a post-0.196 note or archive marker explaining that the no-pushdown statement is historical.
acceptance_criteria: Readers are pointed to current query contract/results for route-dependent execution facts.
The file no longer appears to state current no-pushdown behavior.
findings.suggested_patch_prompt: Add a post-0.196 note to docs/contracts/CURSOR.md explaining that the file is historical and that current route-dependent execution facts live in QUERY_CONTRACT.md and the 0.196 implementation results.
